skill.name: lark-contact
skill.description: 飞书通讯录：查询组织架构、人员信息和搜索员工。获取当前用户或指定用户的详细信息、通过关键词搜索员工（姓名/邮箱/手机号）。当用户需要查看个人信息、查找同事 open_id 或联系方式、按姓名搜索员工、查询部门结构时使用。
skill.summary: Query Lark (Feishu) organization directory, user information, and search employees by name, email, or phone.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 58 potential security issues (external commands, filesystem patterns, high entropy) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains only Markdown documentation with code examples in backticks (not executable code) and relative path references in documentation links. No executable code, network operations, or security risks present. This is legitimate documentation for the Lark CLI tool.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Skill
low_findings.description: This skill contains only Markdown documentation files with no executable code. All detected patterns (backticks, file paths) are part of documentation examples and links, not actual code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading all files - only Markdown documentation with code examples in fenced blocks, no executable content

content.value_statement: Access Lark (Feishu) organization directory directly from Claude. Search employees by name, email, or phone, and retrieve detailed user information including open_id and contact details.
actual_capabilities: Search employees by name, email, or phone number with relevance-ranked results
Retrieve detailed user information including open_id, name, and contact details
Query current user information without authentication parameters
Access organization directory with pagination support for large teams
Format output as JSON or human-readable tables
Handle user_id types: open_id, union_id, and user_id
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary to be installed and configured on the system
Requires valid Lark API authentication credentials (app credentials or user token)
Organization access depends on Lark admin visibility settings
Cannot modify contact information - read-only access
Rate limits apply based on Lark API quotas
Cannot access users outside your organization's visible scope

use_cases.title: Find Colleague Contact Information
use_cases.description: Quickly search for colleagues by name or email to get their open_id and contact details for collaboration tools or API integration.

use_cases.title: Retrieve Current User Profile
use_cases.description: Get your own user information including open_id needed for API calls without looking up credentials manually.

use_cases.title: Organization Directory Queries
use_cases.description: Search through large organizations to find specific team members or verify user IDs before assigning tasks or sending messages.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a User
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the user with email 'john@example.com' in our Lark directory and show me their open_id and name.

prompt_templates.title: Get Current User Info
prompt_templates.prompt: Get my current Lark user information including my open_id and display name.

prompt_templates.title: Search by Name with Pagination
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for users named 'Zhang' and return up to 50 results. If there are more results, show me the page token for the next batch.

prompt_templates.title: Get Specific User Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up user details for open_id 'ou_1234567890' and display the information in a human-readable table format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieving detailed profile for known user ID

output_examples.input: Search for user 'Sarah' in the directory
output_examples.output:
Found 3 users matching 'Sarah':
1. Sarah Chen (open_id: ou_abc123) - sarah@company.com
2. Sarah Johnson (open_id: ou_def456) - sarah.j@company.com
3. Sarah Lee (open_id: ou_ghi789) - sarah.lee@company.com

Your profile:
- Name: John Smith
- Open ID: ou_xyz789
- Email: john.smith@company.com
- Department: Engineering

best_practices: Start with +search-user to find the target user's open_id, then use +get-user with that ID for detailed information
Use the --table flag for human-readable output when debugging, or default JSON format for parsing in scripts
When searching large organizations, use pagination with --page-size and --page-token to avoid overwhelming results
Keep lark-cli authentication credentials secure and use app credentials (tenant_access_token) for organization-wide directory access
anti_patterns: Don't repeatedly call +get-user with different user IDs without first using +search-user to find valid open_ids
Don't set --page-size above 200 as it will be rejected by the API
Don't ignore error 41050 (permission denied) - it usually means the user is outside your organization's visible scope
Don't hardcode user IDs in scripts - always search dynamically as IDs can change

faq.question: Why do I get error 41050 when trying to get user information?
faq.answer: Error 41050 means you lack permission to view that user's information. This typically occurs when the user is outside your organization's visible scope. Contact your admin to adjust your access or use tenant_access_token for broader directory access.

faq.question: What is the difference between open_id, union_id, and user_id?
faq.answer: open_id is app-specific and unique per application. union_id is consistent across all apps in the same organization. user_id is the user's primary ID in the system. Use open_id by default unless you have specific requirements.

faq.question: Do I need to provide authentication for every command?
faq.answer: No. After initial authentication setup via lark-cli config, the credentials are cached. Simply run the contact commands without additional authentication parameters.

faq.question: Can I search for users outside my organization?
faq.answer: No. The contact API only returns users within your organization's visible scope as configured by your Lark admin. Cross-organization directory access is not supported.

faq.question: What happens if a search returns more than 200 results?
faq.answer: Use pagination. The API returns a maximum of 200 results per request (controlled by --page-size). If has_more is true, use the returned page_token in your next request to fetch additional results.

faq.question: Is there a way to get user information without knowing their user_id?
faq.answer: Yes. Use +search-user with a query (name, email, or phone) to find the user first. The search results include open_ids which you can then use with +get-user for detailed information.
